facts.text: Black Sabbath is widely considered the first true heavy metal band, forming in Birmingham, England in 1968.
The Gibson SG became synonymous with heavy metal largely because Tony Iommi of Black Sabbath made it his signature guitar throughout his career.
Marshall amplifiers were originally developed in 1962 by Jim Marshall and have become the most iconic amp brand in metal history.
Metallica's 'Master of Puppets' is frequently cited as the greatest thrash metal album ever recorded.
Rob Halford of Judas Priest is known as the 'Metal God' and pioneered the use of operatic falsetto vocals in heavy metal.
The Mesa Boogie Dual Rectifier amplifier, introduced in 1992, became the go-to amp for modern heavy and nu-metal tones.
Iron Maiden's mascot Eddie the Head has appeared on every album cover since their 1980 debut, becoming one of the most recognizable figures in rock.
Drop D tuning, where the low E string is tuned down a whole step, became widely popular in metal during the 1990s for its heavier, chunkier sound.
The Wacken Open Air festival in Germany is the world's largest heavy metal festival, regularly drawing over 75,000 fans.
Eddie Van Halen's 'Eruption' guitar solo, clocking in at 1 minute 42 seconds, revolutionized electric guitar technique when it was released in 1978.
Tony Iommi lost the tips of two fingers on his right hand in a factory accident, leading him to use lighter gauge strings and create custom fingertip prosthetics that shaped his iconic sound.
The Jackson Rhoads guitar was designed in collaboration with Randy Rhoads and became one of the most popular metal guitar shapes of the 1980s.
Slayer's 'Reign in Blood' runs just 28 minutes and 55 seconds, making it one of the shortest yet most intense thrash albums ever.
Double bass drum pedals allow a single drummer to play rapid bass drum patterns using both feet on one bass drum, a technique essential to thrash and death metal.
Ronnie James Dio is widely credited with popularizing the 'devil horns' hand gesture in heavy metal, which he learned from his Italian grandmother as a ward against the evil eye.
The Gibson Flying V, originally released in 1958, was considered a commercial failure until metal guitarists adopted it in the late 1960s and 1970s.
Motörhead's Lemmy Kilmister played bass through guitar amplifiers — specifically Marshall stacks — to achieve his signature distorted, aggressive tone.
Led Zeppelin's 'Whole Lotta Love' riff, written by Jimmy Page, is one of the most recognized guitar riffs in rock and metal history.
The BC Rich Warlock guitar, with its aggressive pointed body shape, became a visual icon of 1980s heavy metal.
Judas Priest's 1980 album 'British Steel' helped define the New Wave of British Heavy Metal and is considered one of the genre's essential records.
Kirk Hammett of Metallica is known for his extensive use of the wah pedal, particularly the Dunlop Cry Baby, which has become part of his signature lead tone.
Deep Purple's 'Smoke on the Water' riff is often the first thing new guitarists learn and was recorded using a Fender Stratocaster through a Marshall Major amp.
The Peavey 5150 amplifier was co-designed with Eddie Van Halen in 1992 and became one of the most popular high-gain amps in metal.
Ozzy Osbourne was fired from Black Sabbath in 1979 due to substance abuse issues, leading to his hugely successful solo career.
Tool's album 'Lateralus' incorporates the Fibonacci sequence into its song structure and time signatures, blending progressive metal with mathematical precision.
ESP guitars became the brand of choice for thrash metal players in the late 1980s, with Kirk Hammett and James Hetfield both playing ESP models.
The 'Big Four' of thrash metal — Metallica, Megadeth, Slayer, and Anthrax — first performed together on the same stage at the Sonisphere Festival in 2010.
Dave Mustaine was Metallica's original lead guitarist before being fired in 1983, after which he formed Megadeth.
Orange amplifiers, known for their distinctive orange tolex covering, have been used by metal bands ranging from Sleep to Mastodon for their warm, thick overdrive.
Dimebag Darrell of Pantera was known for using a Dean ML guitar and a Randall amplifier to create his crushing groove metal tone.
Iron Maiden's 'The Number of the Beast' album caused widespread controversy upon its 1982 release due to its title and cover art.
Mosh pits originated in the early 1980s hardcore punk scene but became a staple of heavy metal concerts by the mid-1980s.
Drop C tuning, two whole steps below standard on the low string, is commonly used in metalcore and modern metal for its deep, heavy sound.
System of a Down's 'Toxicity' album debuted at number one on the Billboard 200 in 2001, bringing Armenian influences into mainstream metal.
The Marshall JCM800 amplifier, introduced in 1981, became the defining amp of 1980s metal and hard rock.
Def Leppard's drummer Rick Allen lost his left arm in a car accident in 1984 but continued drumming using a custom electronic drum kit.
Blue Öyster Cult's '(Don't Fear) The Reaper' is one of the earliest songs often associated with proto-metal, released in 1976.
Ibanez guitars, particularly the RG series, became hugely popular with metal shredders in the 1980s and 1990s due to their thin necks and floating tremolo systems.
Metallica's 'Black Album' spent four consecutive weeks at number one and has sold over 16 million copies in the US alone.
The distortion pedal is the most fundamental effects pedal in metal, with classics like the Boss DS-1 and Pro Co RAT being staples of many pedalboards.
Scorpions' 'Rock You Like a Hurricane' became one of the defining metal anthems of the 1980s.
Ghost's vocalist Tobias Forge performs as 'Papa Emeritus' (and later 'Cardinal Copia'), wearing elaborate costumes and face paint inspired by Catholic imagery.
Heavy gauge strings, typically .011 to .054 or heavier, are preferred by many metal guitarists for their tighter feel and heavier tone in drop tunings.
Randy Rhoads, Ozzy Osbourne's guitarist, was classically trained and brought neoclassical elements to heavy metal before his tragic death in 1982 at age 25.
Lamb of God formed in Richmond, Virginia in 1994 under the name Burn the Priest before changing their name in 1999.
The wah pedal, originally designed in 1966, became a key part of metal lead guitar tone, used extensively by players like Kirk Hammett and Dimebag Darrell.
W.A.S.P. were one of the most controversial metal bands of the 1980s, with their live shows featuring pyrotechnics and theatrical shock elements.
Mastodon's 'Crack the Skye' is a concept album dealing with astral projection, wormholes, and Rasputin, showcasing progressive metal at its most ambitious.
The Floyd Rose locking tremolo system, invented in 1977, revolutionized metal guitar by allowing extreme dive bombs and pitch manipulation without going out of tune.
Testament, often called the unofficial 'fifth member' of thrash's Big Four, released their debut 'The Legacy' in 1987.
Avenged Sevenfold's album 'City of Evil' in 2005 marked a shift from metalcore to a more traditional heavy metal sound with dual guitar harmonies.
The Marshall Plexi amplifier, used by early metal pioneers, got its nickname from the plexiglass panel on its front face.
Zakk Wylde became Ozzy Osbourne's guitarist in 1987 at age 19 and is known for playing a Gibson Les Paul with his iconic bullseye paint job.
Download Festival at Donington Park, UK, is one of the premier rock and metal festivals in the world, succeeding the legendary Monsters of Rock.
Gojira, from Bayonne, France, are known for their environmentally conscious lyrics and technically demanding progressive death metal.
The flanger effect pedal creates a swooshing, jet-like sound and was notably used by Eddie Van Halen on tracks like 'Unchained' using an MXR Flanger.
Dio's 'Holy Diver' album, released in 1983, is considered one of the greatest traditional heavy metal albums ever made.
Nu-metal emerged in the mid-1990s, blending metal with hip-hop, funk, and grunge elements, with bands like Korn, Limp Bizkit, and Linkin Park.
Power metal bands like Helloween, Blind Guardian, and DragonForce are characterized by fast tempos, soaring vocals, and fantasy-themed lyrics.
Spiritbox, fronted by Courtney LaPlante, blends djent, metalcore, and progressive metal and became one of the most talked-about metal bands of the 2020s.
James Hetfield of Metallica uses extremely heavy pick attack with thick tortex picks (typically 1.14mm) to achieve his aggressive rhythm tone.
Twisted Sister's 'We're Not Gonna Take It' became an anthem of rebellion in the 1980s and was featured in their PMRC Senate hearing controversy.
The bass guitar plays a crucial role in metal, with players like Cliff Burton, Steve Harris, and Geezer Butler elevating the instrument beyond simple root note patterns.
Ozzfest, created by Ozzy and Sharon Osbourne in 1996, became one of the most important touring metal festivals in North America.
Doom metal, pioneered by bands like Candlemass and Saint Vitus, features slow, heavy riffs often tuned down to C standard or lower.
The ESP Eclipse guitar, based on the Les Paul single-cutaway design, is popular among metal guitarists for its combination of classic aesthetics and modern playability.
Megadeth's 'Rust in Peace' is often cited as the pinnacle of technical thrash metal, featuring the guitar work of Dave Mustaine and Marty Friedman.
Exodus, one of the original Bay Area thrash bands, helped create the thrash metal movement alongside Metallica in the early 1980s.
Symphonic metal bands like Nightwish, Epica, and Within Temptation combine heavy guitar riffs with orchestral arrangements and operatic female vocals.
The Mesa Boogie Mark IIC+ amplifier is one of the most sought-after metal amps ever made, used by John Petrucci and James Hetfield.
Mötley Crüe's autobiography 'The Dirt' detailed some of the most outrageous rock and roll stories of the 1980s LA metal scene.
Seven-string guitars, popularized by Steve Vai and later adopted by bands like Korn and Dream Theater, extended the low range available to metal guitarists.
Black Sabbath's self-titled debut album was released on Friday the 13th of February 1970, a deliberate choice by the band.
Groove metal, pioneered by Pantera and Exhodus in the early 1990s, emphasizes slower, grooving riffs over thrash metal's speed.
The denim vest (or 'battle jacket') covered in patches and pins from favorite bands is one of the most enduring fashion staples of metal culture.
Overkill, from New Jersey, have been consistently releasing thrash metal albums since 1985, making them one of the most prolific bands in the genre.
The Ibanez JEM guitar, designed for Steve Vai, features a distinctive monkey grip handle cut into the body and a Lion's Claw tremolo cavity.
Bruce Dickinson of Iron Maiden is also a licensed commercial airline pilot who has flown the band's touring plane, Ed Force One.
Metalcore blends metal with hardcore punk, with bands like Killswitch Engage, As I Lay Dying, and Trivium gaining mainstream success in the 2000s.
The Boss MT-2 Metal Zone pedal, despite being mocked by some guitarists, is one of the best-selling distortion pedals ever made.
Van Halen's debut album in 1978 revolutionized rock guitar with Eddie Van Halen's two-handed tapping technique.
Led Zeppelin's 'Stairway to Heaven' was famously never released as a single despite being one of the most requested songs in radio history.
Hellfest in Clisson, France, has grown to become one of Europe's largest metal and extreme music festivals since its founding in 2006.
The Jackson Soloist guitar, originally a custom shop model, became one of the first 'superstrat' guitars designed specifically for metal playing.
Pantera's 'Cowboys from Hell' in 1990 marked the band's transformation from glam metal to the groove metal sound that would define their legacy.
Bloodstock Open Air in Derbyshire, England, is the UK's largest independent metal festival, running annually since 2001.
Drop B tuning is commonly used in modern metal and deathcore, requiring baritone-scale guitars or very heavy string gauges to maintain string tension.
Anthrax were unique among the Big Four of thrash for incorporating elements of hip-hop, ska, and humor into their music.
The Randall Warhead amplifier was Dimebag Darrell's signature amp, designed to replicate his crushing Pantera tone.
Cliff Burton, Metallica's original bassist, was known for using distortion and wah effects on his bass guitar, creating a unique lead bass style.
Rock am Ring and its sister festival Rock im Park in Germany are twin festivals held simultaneously each June, featuring major metal and rock acts.
Progressive metal bands like Dream Theater, Opeth, and Between the Buried and Me combine metal's heaviness with complex time signatures and extended compositions.
The EMG 81 active pickup is one of the most widely used pickups in metal, known for its high output and tight, focused tone.
Iron Maiden's Steve Harris plays a Fender Precision Bass with flatwound strings and a galloping fingerstyle technique that defines the band's sound.
Judas Priest's 'Painkiller' album in 1990 is considered one of the fastest and most intense traditional metal albums ever recorded.
Dimebag Darrell was tragically shot and killed on stage in Columbus, Ohio on December 8, 2004, while performing with Damageplan.
The 'scooped mids' EQ setting — boosting bass and treble while cutting midrange — became a popular metal guitar tone in the late 1980s and 1990s.
Ozzy Osbourne infamously bit the head off a live bat thrown on stage during a concert in Des Moines, Iowa in 1982, thinking it was a rubber toy.
Lamb of God's vocalist Randy Blythe was arrested in Prague in 2012 on manslaughter charges related to a fan's death at a 2010 concert; he was later acquitted.
The Gibson Explorer, like the Flying V, was initially a commercial failure in the late 1950s but was later embraced by metal guitarists including James Hetfield.
Metallica's 'Ride the Lightning' was the band's second album and showed a significant evolution in songwriting complexity.
The 'wall of death' is a concert phenomenon where the crowd splits into two halves and charges at each other when the music kicks in.
Ernie Ball Slinky strings are among the most popular string brands in metal, with many players using their 'Not Even Slinky' heavy gauge sets (.012-.056).
Deep Purple's 'Machine Head' album, recorded in Montreux, Switzerland, was inspired by a fire at the Montreux Casino during a Frank Zappa concert.
Black Sabbath tuned down their instruments to C# standard on many songs, pioneering the use of detuned guitars in heavy music.
The PMRC (Parents Music Resource Center) hearings in 1985 targeted metal bands, leading to the 'Parental Advisory' sticker on albums.
System of a Down have been on hiatus from recording new studio material since 2005's 'Mezmerize' and 'Hypnotize' double album, despite reunion tours.
The Dunlop Cry Baby wah pedal, first produced in 1966, remains one of the most popular effects pedals among metal lead guitarists.
Mastodon's four-album concept spanning the four classical elements (fire, water, earth, air) is one of modern metal's most ambitious projects.
The BC Rich Mockingbird guitar was a favorite of metal guitarists in the 1980s for its distinctive body shape and aggressive styling.
Ghost won the Grammy Award for Best Metal Performance in 2016 for their song 'Cirice'.
Ride cymbal bell hits and china cymbals are essential components of the aggressive drumming style found in thrash and death metal.
Def Leppard's 'Hysteria' album took over three years to record and produce, eventually selling over 20 million copies worldwide.
Tapping technique on guitar, popularized by Eddie Van Halen, involves using fingers of the picking hand to hammer on and pull off notes on the fretboard.
Motörhead's Lemmy Kilmister stood at a 45-degree angle looking up at his microphone, a unique stage posture that became his trademark.
The term 'heavy metal' in a musical context is often attributed to Steppenwolf's 1968 song 'Born to Be Wild,' which included the phrase 'heavy metal thunder.'
Dave Mustaine was kicked out of Metallica on a bus ride to New York in 1983, and he famously vowed to form a band that was heavier and faster.
The Orange Rockerverb amplifier is known for its warm, saturated overdrive and has been adopted by stoner and doom metal bands worldwide.
Whammy bars (tremolo arms) are used extensively in metal for dive bombs, squeals, and pitch manipulation, particularly with Floyd Rose equipped guitars.
Pantera's 'Vulgar Display of Power' album cover features a man being punched in the face, captured with a high-speed camera.
Avenged Sevenfold's drummer The Rev (James Owen Sullivan) died in 2009 at age 28; Mike Portnoy of Dream Theater filled in for recording their next album.
Noise gates are essential pedals in metal setups, cutting out unwanted hum and feedback between riffs when using high-gain amplifiers.
The New Wave of British Heavy Metal (NWOBHM) in the late 1970s and early 1980s spawned Iron Maiden, Def Leppard, Saxon, and Diamond Head.
Seymour Duncan pickups, particularly the JB (Jazz/Blues) humbucker, are among the most recorded pickups in metal history.
Tool's 'Fear Inoculum', released in 2019 after a 13-year gap, debuted at number one on the Billboard 200.
Tony Iommi's use of power chords and tritone intervals on Black Sabbath's debut helped create the 'evil' sound that became central to metal.
Metallica and the San Francisco Symphony Orchestra collaborated on the 'S&M' album in 1999, blending thrash metal with full orchestral arrangements.
The Peavey 6505 (formerly 5150 II after Eddie Van Halen's departure) remains one of the best-selling high-gain amplifiers for modern metal.
Judas Priest were one of the first metal bands to fully embrace a leather and studs aesthetic, establishing a lasting visual style for the genre.
Baritone guitars with longer scale lengths (27"+) are used in modern metal to accommodate extremely low tunings while maintaining string tension.
Iron Maiden's 'Powerslave' album tour featured one of the most elaborate stage shows of the 1980s, inspired by Egyptian pharaoh imagery.
Gojira's Joe Duplantier developed a signature picking technique called 'pick scraping' combined with natural harmonics to create eerie, whale-like sounds.
The Digitech Whammy pedal, which shifts pitch up or down, became famous in metal through Tom Morello of Rage Against the Machine and Dimebag Darrell.
Van Halen's 'brown sound' — Eddie's signature guitar tone — was achieved using a modified Marshall Super Lead amplifier and a Variac voltage regulator.
Sunn O))) amplifiers, originally made by Sunn Musical Equipment, became iconic in drone and doom metal for their massive, earth-shaking low end.
Megadeth's 'Peace Sells... but Who's Buying?' bass intro by Dave Ellefson became the theme for MTV News in the late 1980s.
Extended range guitars with 8 strings, popularized by bands like Meshuggah and Animals as Leaders, push the low-end boundary of metal even further.
The 'devil horns' hand sign, popularized by Dio, is sometimes confused with the 'I Love You' sign in American Sign Language, which includes the thumb.
Slayer's Kerry King plays BC Rich guitars and is known for his aggressive, atonal soloing style that helped define thrash metal lead guitar.
The Marshall JVM410H is a modern high-gain amplifier with four channels, popular among metal guitarists who need versatile tones.
Ozzy Osbourne's 'Blizzard of Ozz' album launched his solo career and introduced the world to guitarist Randy Rhoads.
Drum triggers are electronic sensors attached to acoustic drums that send a signal to a sound module, ensuring consistent tone at high speeds in metal drumming.
Death metal vocal techniques, including guttural growling and 'cookie monster' vocals, were pioneered by bands like Death, Possessed, and Morbid Angel in the 1980s.
The Boss HM-2 Heavy Metal pedal, discontinued in 1991, became legendary for its association with Swedish death metal bands like Entombed and Dismember.
Arch Enemy became one of the first major metal bands with a female growling vocalist when Angela Gossow joined in 2000.
Scalloped fretboards, where the wood between frets is scooped out, are used by some metal guitarists like Yngwie Malmsteen for easier bending and vibrato.
Metallica's 'Kill 'Em All' in 1983 is considered one of the first thrash metal albums and set the template for the genre.
The Tube Screamer overdrive pedal is often used in metal as a 'boost' in front of a high-gain amp to tighten the low end and add midrange presence.
Scorpions' 'Blackout' album cover, featuring a person with forks in their eyes, was one of the most controversial album covers of the 1980s.
Zakk Wylde founded Black Label Society in 1998 and is known for his use of pinch harmonics, which he calls 'squeals.'
Active pickups like EMGs require a 9-volt battery and produce a hotter, more compressed signal compared to passive pickups.
Kreator, Sodom, and Destruction form the 'Big Three' (Teutonic Three) of German thrash metal.
The Gibson Les Paul Custom, often called the 'Black Beauty,' has been used by metal guitarists from Randy Rhoads to Adam Jones of Tool.
Randy Rhoads' solo on 'Crazy Train' is one of the most recognizable guitar solos in metal, blending classical and rock techniques.
Mudvayne's bassist Ryan Martinie is known for his complex, jazz-influenced bass lines in a nu-metal context.
The Dunlop Jazz III pick is favored by many metal shredders for its small size, sharp tip, and precision picking capability.
Dio's band formed after he left Black Sabbath in 1982, releasing 'Holy Diver' as their debut album the following year.
D-Tuna devices allow Floyd Rose-equipped guitars to switch from standard tuning to drop D instantly without affecting the other strings.
The 'wall of sound' production technique, stacking multiple guitar tracks, became standard in metal recording during the 1990s.
Sepultura from Brazil brought thrash metal to South America and incorporated indigenous Brazilian percussion into their later work.
John Petrucci of Dream Theater uses Ernie Ball Music Man signature guitars with custom DiMarzio pickups and Mesa Boogie amplifiers.
Headbanging is believed to have originated at a Led Zeppelin concert in 1969, though the term wasn't widely used until the 1980s.
The Jackson King V guitar, played by Dave Mustaine and Robbin Crosby, is one of the most aggressive-looking metal guitar designs.
Nightwish pioneered symphonic metal by combining a full orchestra and operatic soprano vocals with heavy metal instrumentation.
Iron Maiden released 'The Number of the Beast' in 1982, marking Bruce Dickinson's debut as the band's vocalist and their commercial breakthrough.
Cab simulation technology allows metal guitarists to record direct without a traditional speaker cabinet, using impulse responses (IRs) of classic cabs.
Slayer co-founder Jeff Hanneman died of liver failure in 2013, and the band continued as a trio before their farewell tour.
The phase shifter effect pedal creates a sweeping, spacey sound and was used by Eddie Van Halen on numerous Van Halen tracks.
Mötley Crüe's Tommy Lee performed a drum solo while his kit rotated 360 degrees on a mechanical arm above the crowd.
White Zombie, led by Rob Zombie, blended industrial metal with horror movie samples and visual aesthetics in the early 1990s.
The pitch shifter pedal allows guitarists to harmonize with themselves in real time, a technique used extensively in modern progressive metal.
Bullet for My Valentine helped spearhead the Welsh metal scene, blending thrash influences with metalcore and melodic elements.
Humbucking pickups, invented by Seth Lover at Gibson in 1955, reject 60-cycle hum and produce a thicker, warmer tone favored in metal.
Opeth seamlessly blend progressive rock, death metal, and acoustic folk music, with frontman Mikael Åkerfeldt handling both clean vocals and growls.
The Mesa Boogie Mark V amplifier is known as a 'Swiss Army knife' amp, offering everything from clean jazz tones to crushing high-gain metal.
Venom's 1982 album 'Black Metal' gave the black metal subgenre its name, though the sound would be further developed by Norwegian bands in the early 1990s.
Reverb is used strategically in metal production — tight or no reverb for rhythm guitars, and longer reverb on lead guitar solos for ambiance.
Metallica's Lars Ulrich was originally a tennis prodigy before choosing to pursue music full-time.
The Charvel San Dimas guitar was one of the original 'superstrat' models, designed in the early 1980s for fast-playing, high-gain metal applications.
Deftones blend metal with shoegaze and dream pop elements, creating a subgenre sometimes called 'atmospheric metal.'
String gauge directly affects playability in metal — heavier strings provide more tension and fuller tone but require more finger strength.
Dave Grohl played drums for several metal and punk bands, including Scream and a brief stint with QOTSA, before focusing on Foo Fighters.
Killswitch Engage's 'The End of Heartache' helped bring metalcore into mainstream consciousness in 2004.
The Maxon OD808, the original circuit behind the Ibanez Tube Screamer, is prized by metal guitarists for its mid-boost characteristics.
Devin Townsend is known for his use of open C tuning and the 'wall of sound' production technique in his solo and Strapping Young Lad work.
Black Sabbath's 'Paranoid' was written in about 25 minutes as a filler track but became one of the band's biggest hits.
Meshuggah pioneered the 'djent' subgenre with their polyrhythmic, heavily palm-muted guitar tone using 8-string guitars.
Celestion Vintage 30 speakers are the most commonly used speakers in metal guitar cabinets, known for their pronounced midrange.
The Kiesel/Carvin guitar company offers fully customizable guitars popular in the progressive and modern metal community.
Trivium's Matt Heafy plays ESP and Epiphone signature models and has become one of the most visible metal guitarists on social media.
Palm muting — resting the picking hand on the strings near the bridge — is the most fundamental rhythm guitar technique in metal.
Ronnie James Dio passed away on May 16, 2010 from stomach cancer, and a hologram of him toured with his former bandmates in 2017.
Behringer's affordable guitar pedals have made classic metal effects accessible to beginners, though purists debate their tonal quality.
The Big Four of thrash metal collectively sold over 200 million albums worldwide.
Alice in Chains blended heavy metal with grunge, creating some of the darkest and heaviest music of the early 1990s Seattle scene.
Multi-scale (fan-fret) guitars provide different scale lengths for each string, improving intonation and string tension in extended range instruments.
Corey Taylor of Slipknot uses multiple vocal styles including clean singing, screaming, and rapping, often within the same song.
The Marshall Valvestate amplifier line offered the Marshall name at a lower price point and was popular with beginner and intermediate metal guitarists.
Queensrÿche's 'Operation: Mindcrime' is widely regarded as one of the greatest concept albums in heavy metal history.
Guitar picks come in various materials for metal playing — Tortex, Ultex, and metal picks each offer different attack and tone characteristics.
Type O Negative blended gothic metal, doom, and dark humor, with Peter Steele's deep baritone vocals becoming their signature sound.
The Friedman BE-100 amplifier, designed by amp guru Dave Friedman, is based on hot-rodded Marshall circuits and is used by many modern metal players.
Lemmy Kilmister of Motörhead played Rickenbacker basses throughout most of his career, an unusual choice for a metal musician.
Between the Buried and Me's 'Colors' album showcases progressive metalcore with seamless transitions between brutal death metal and jazz fusion passages.
Tuner pedals are the unsung heroes of metal pedalboards — the Boss TU-3 and TC Electronic Polytune are the most commonly seen.
Slipknot's debut album cover featured the nine members wearing matching red jumpsuits and individual custom masks.
The harmonic minor scale is extensively used in neoclassical metal by guitarists like Yngwie Malmsteen, Randy Rhoads, and Ritchie Blackmore.
Bullet belts, studded wristbands, and band t-shirts have been staples of metal fashion since the early 1980s New Wave of British Heavy Metal.
Peavey's Wolfgang amplifier was designed with Eddie Van Halen after the 5150 partnership ended, though the 5150 design continued as the 6505.
Bands like In Flames, Dark Tranquillity, and At the Gates pioneered melodic death metal in Gothenburg, Sweden in the early 1990s.
The 'brown note' — an infrasonic frequency rumored to cause involuntary bowel movements — has been a running joke in metal culture, though it doesn't actually exist.
Gojira's 'From Mars to Sirius' includes the song 'Flying Whales,' which has become one of the most iconic progressive death metal tracks.
Locking tuners help maintain tuning stability in metal playing, especially during aggressive strumming and string bending.
Judas Priest were accused of embedding subliminal messages in their music during a 1990 trial; they were ultimately acquitted.
The Jackson Dinky guitar is a slightly smaller superstrat that became hugely popular in shred and metal scenes for its lightweight body and fast neck.
Arch Enemy's Michael Amott plays custom Caparison and Dean guitars with a distinctive melodic lead style rooted in the Gothenburg sound.
The delay pedal is used in metal leads to create a sense of space and depth, with the MXR Carbon Copy and Boss DD-7 being popular choices.
Sabaton write songs exclusively about historical battles and warfare, creating a unique niche in the power metal genre.
Download Festival was first held at Donington Park in 2003, replacing the Monsters of Rock festival that had run since 1980.
Alternate picking — strict down-up-down-up motion — is the foundation of speed picking technique used by metal shredders.
Megadeth's 'Countdown to Extinction' debuted at number two on the Billboard 200 in 1992, a commercial peak for the band.
Bullet for My Valentine's guitarist Michael Paget plays ESP signature models featuring a unique single-cutaway design.
Volume swells — using the guitar's volume knob or a volume pedal to fade notes in — create a violin-like effect used in metal ballads and ambient passages.
Children of Bodom's Alexi Laiho was known for blending neoclassical shred with melodic death metal on his Jackson and ESP signature guitars.
Rammstein's pyrotechnic-heavy live shows are among the most elaborate in metal, featuring flamethrowers, fireworks, and burning stage props.
High-output passive pickups like the DiMarzio Super Distortion bridge the gap between traditional passive and modern active pickup designs.
Blind Guardian are known as the 'Bards of Metal' for their Tolkien-inspired lyrics and multi-layered vocal harmonies.
The Maxon SD-9 Sonic Distortion pedal has been used by thrash and death metal bands for its aggressive, crunchy overdrive sound.
Anthrax's collaboration with Public Enemy on 'Bring the Noise' in 1991 was a groundbreaking fusion of thrash metal and hip-hop.
Trem-picking (tremolo picking) — rapidly alternate picking a single note — is a technique used extensively in black and death metal.
Motörhead's 'Ace of Spades' is one of the most recognizable songs in metal, often played at the 11th hour in tribute to Lemmy.
The Axe-Fx by Fractal Audio is a digital amp modeler that has been adopted by many touring metal bands as a replacement for traditional amplifiers.
Pantera's Phil Anselmo went through severe back problems and addiction issues that nearly destroyed the band in their final years.
Coated strings such as Elixir Nanowebs are popular in metal for their extended lifespan, especially important for players who sweat heavily during performances.
Lamb of God's 'Ashes of the Wake' album is a politically charged groove metal masterpiece that reached number 27 on the Billboard 200.
The looper pedal has become a useful practice tool for metal guitarists, allowing them to record rhythm parts and practice soloing over them.
Steel Panther is a comedic metal band that parodies the excess of 1980s hair metal with virtuoso musicianship.
Black Label Society's Zakk Wylde plays his Gibson Les Paul through a Marshall JCM800 as the core of his punishing, bluesy metal tone.
Metallica famously sued Napster in 2000, becoming one of the first major artists to fight against illegal music downloading.
Chorus pedals create a shimmering, doubled sound and were used by bands like Metallica on their 'Black Album' clean passages.
Mastodon's Brent Hinds plays a custom Epiphone Flying V and blends Southern rock influences with progressive metal.
Thin Lizzy's dual guitar harmonies, pioneered by Scott Gorham and Brian Robertson, directly influenced Iron Maiden's twin guitar attack.
An EQ pedal in the effects loop is a common metal setup trick, allowing precise control over the amp's tone without affecting the gain structure.
The Rockfield Studios in Wales is where Black Sabbath, Judas Priest, and Motörhead recorded some of their most iconic albums.
Death, led by Chuck Schuldiner, is considered the first true death metal band, with their 1987 debut 'Scream Bloody Gore' defining the genre.
Carbon fiber guitar picks are gaining popularity in metal for their durability and bright attack compared to traditional nylon picks.
Rob Zombie transitioned from White Zombie frontman to successful horror film director while maintaining his metal career.
The Orange Thunderverb amplifier combines vintage-style British crunch with modern high-gain capabilities favored by doom and sludge metal bands.
Korn's debut album in 1994 is considered the album that launched the nu-metal movement, featuring detuned 7-string Ibanez guitars.
Pickup height adjustment is a crucial but often overlooked factor in metal guitar tone — pickups too close to the strings can cause unwanted magnetic pull.
Dream Theater's John Petrucci is consistently voted among the greatest metal guitarists alive and helped popularize the 7-string guitar in progressive metal.
The Epiphone Les Paul has served as an affordable entry point for metal guitarists who want the Les Paul tone without the Gibson price tag.
Heaven and Hell was the name used by Black Sabbath's Dio-era lineup when they reunited and toured in the late 2000s.
Natural harmonics at the 5th, 7th, and 12th frets are used extensively in metal for creating high-pitched bell-like tones and squeals.
Ozzy Osbourne's reality TV show 'The Osbournes' debuted in 2002 and introduced the metal icon to a mainstream audience.
The neural DSP Quad Cortex and Line 6 Helix digital modelers have become standard touring equipment for metal bands in the 2020s.
Helloween's 'Keeper of the Seven Keys' albums essentially created European power metal as a genre in 1987-1988.
Bathory, from Sweden, pioneered both black metal and Viking metal, with Quorthon's one-man project influencing countless metal subgenres.
The compression pedal is used in metal to even out dynamics, particularly for bass guitar and clean guitar tones requiring consistent volume.
Trivium blends thrash, metalcore, and progressive metal, with their album 'Shogun' being praised as one of the best metal albums of the 2000s.
Fender guitars, traditionally associated with blues and country, have been adopted by some metal guitarists — Jim Root of Slipknot plays Fender signature models.
Wacken Open Air has been held annually since 1990 in the small village of Wacken, Germany, population approximately 1,800.
String dampeners (fretwraps) are increasingly used by metal guitarists to mute unwanted string noise, especially on extended range guitars.
The concept album has a long tradition in metal, from Iron Maiden's 'Seventh Son of a Seventh Son' to Mastodon's 'Leviathan.'
Stainless steel guitar frets are becoming standard in metal guitars, offering far greater durability than traditional nickel-silver frets under heavy playing.
Spiritbox's 'Eternal Blue' debuted at number 13 on the Billboard 200 in 2021, a remarkable achievement for a modern progressive metalcore band.
The octave pedal, particularly the Electro-Harmonix POG, is used in metal to thicken guitar parts by adding an octave above or below the played note.
Cannibal Corpse have sold over 2 million albums worldwide despite receiving virtually no radio airplay or mainstream media coverage.
Led Zeppelin IV, featuring 'Stairway to Heaven' and 'Black Dog,' has sold over 37 million copies worldwide.
Different pick thicknesses serve different purposes in metal — thin picks flex for rhythmic strumming, while thick picks (1.0mm+) provide precision for lead work.
Volbeat blend heavy metal with rockabilly and Elvis-inspired vocals, creating a unique sound that has earned them massive success in Europe.
Compound radius fretboards (flatter at higher frets) are popular on modern metal guitars, allowing comfortable chord playing at low frets and easy bending up high.
Clutch blend stoner metal, blues rock, and funk, building a devoted following through relentless touring.
Machine Head's 'Burn My Eyes' debut album in 1994 combined thrash and groove metal and became a benchmark for the Bay Area metal scene.
The bridge position pickup on electric guitars provides the brighter, more aggressive tone preferred for metal rhythm playing.
Halford vocalist Rob Halford came out as gay in 1998, becoming one of the first major metal musicians to publicly discuss his sexuality.
Acoustic intros and interludes are a common device in metal, with Metallica's 'One,' 'Fade to Black,' and 'Nothing Else Matters' being prime examples.
The Schecter Hellraiser guitar line, featuring EMG pickups and set necks, became a popular affordable option for modern metal in the 2000s.
Five Finger Death Punch became one of the most commercially successful metal bands of the 2010s despite mixed critical reception.
Gain stacking — running multiple overdrive or distortion pedals — is a technique used by some metal guitarists to achieve extreme levels of saturation.
Manowar hold the Guinness World Record for the loudest concert performance, reaching 139 dB in 2008.
The 'gallop' picking pattern — a syncopated rhythm of down-down-up — is the signature technique of Iron Maiden's guitar playing.
Exodus guitarist Gary Holt joined Slayer in 2011 to replace Jeff Hanneman and remained with the band until their final show.
The Chapman Stick and other touch-style instruments have found a niche in progressive metal for their ability to play bass and melody simultaneously.
Slipknot's nine-member lineup includes a DJ and two percussionists, making them one of the largest metal bands in terms of members.
Every Time I Die helped bridge the gap between metalcore and Southern rock, with chaotic, unpredictable live performances.
Flatwound bass strings produce a thumpy, rounded tone used in doom metal, while roundwound strings offer the bright, clanky sound preferred in thrash.
Yngwie Malmsteen's 'Rising Force' in 1984 essentially created the neoclassical metal genre, blending Baroque-inspired guitar work with heavy metal.
Black Sabbath's final concert took place in their hometown of Birmingham on February 4, 2017.
Killswitch Engage featured two different lead vocalists — Jesse Leach and Howard Jones — with Leach returning in 2012 after an 11-year absence.
Sustainiac and Fernandes Sustainer pickups are neck-position devices that create infinite sustain for metal leads and ambient textures.
W.A.S.P. frontman Blackie Lawless famously had a circular saw blade codpiece as part of his stage outfit.
Djent, characterized by heavily palm-muted, rhythmically complex guitar patterns, originated from Meshuggah and was popularized by Periphery and TesseracT.
The Bogner Uberschall amplifier is a boutique high-gain amp known for its tight, aggressive metal tone.
Saxon were one of the leading bands of the NWOBHM alongside Iron Maiden and Def Leppard but never achieved the same level of commercial success.
Ear protection at metal concerts has become increasingly normalized, with custom-molded musician's earplugs reducing volume without sacrificing sound quality.
Electric guitar strings for metal are typically made of nickel-plated steel, though some players prefer pure nickel for a warmer vintage tone.
Paradise Lost pioneered gothic metal in the early 1990s alongside Type O Negative, blending doom metal with dark romantic atmospheres.
Metallica's 'St. Anger' album in 2003 was controversial for its raw production and the absence of guitar solos.
Strap locks are essential metal guitar hardware, preventing the guitar from falling off the strap during aggressive stage movement.
Ghost's albums follow a narrative arc with each new Papa Emeritus incarnation representing a different era of the fictional clergy.
Capo use is rare in metal, though some bands use partial capos or open tunings for unique voicings.
The 'Metal Horns' hand sign involves extending the index and pinky fingers while folding down the middle and ring fingers with the thumb.
Avenged Sevenfold's Synyster Gates is known for his jazz-influenced shred style and plays Schecter signature guitars with Sustaniac pickups.
The Engl Powerball amplifier is a German-made high-gain amp that has been adopted by many European metal bands.
Power chords — two-note chords consisting of the root and fifth — are the building blocks of virtually all metal rhythm guitar.
The 'circle pit' is a concert phenomenon where a large group of concertgoers runs in a circle in the mosh pit area.
DiMarzio pickups, including the D Activator and Crunch Lab models, are popular alternatives to EMGs in modern metal guitars.
Gwar, the intergalactic warrior band, perform in elaborate alien costumes and spray fake blood on their audiences.
The harmonic pinch technique, where the thumb grazes the string right after picking, creates a high-pitched 'squeal' that is a staple of metal lead playing.
Pantera's Dimebag Darrell used a signature Washburn Dime guitar before switching to Dean guitars later in his career.
Fishman Fluence pickups offer switchable voicings in a single pickup, allowing metal guitarists to go from vintage to modern tones at the push of a button.
Megadeth has gone through over 20 lineup changes since their formation, with Dave Mustaine being the only constant member.
The Bogner Ecstasy amplifier is used by metal and hard rock players who want a more articulate, less compressed high-gain tone.
Coheed and Cambria blend progressive metal with emo and pop-punk influences, telling a science fiction narrative across their albums.
The vibrato technique in metal ranges from subtle finger vibrato to wide, aggressive wrist vibrato made famous by players like Zakk Wylde.
Testament's Alex Skolnick is one of the most technically proficient thrash metal guitarists and also performs in a jazz trio.
Ozzy Osbourne's 'No More Tears' was his most commercially successful solo album, going quadruple platinum in the US.
Dead tuning (or 'drop dead' tuning) involves tuning all strings down significantly, sometimes to drop A or lower, in extreme metal styles.
Iron Maiden's 'Aces High' opens with the sound of Winston Churchill's famous 'We shall fight them on the beaches' speech.
The Suhr Modern guitar, with its roasted maple neck, is an increasingly popular choice in progressive metal circles.
Metal album art has been elevated to fine art status, with artists like Derek Riggs (Iron Maiden), Ed Repka (Megadeth), and Dan Seagrave (death metal) achieving legendary status.
Muddy Waters' and Robert Johnson's blues heavily influenced early metal, with Black Sabbath, Led Zeppelin, and Deep Purple all starting as blues-rock bands.
The chorus effect on bass guitar, as heard on Metallica's 'Anesthesia (Pulling Teeth),' adds shimmer and depth to the instrument.
Within Temptation and Lacuna Coil brought gothic and symphonic metal to mainstream European audiences in the 2000s.
Guitar center and music stores traditionally see spikes in electric guitar sales after major metal tours and album releases.
Jason Newsted was infamously given almost inaudible bass in the mix on Metallica's '...And Justice for All' album.
The 'spaghetti headstock' is a distinctive Jackson guitar feature with sharply pointed, offset tuning pegs.
Judas Priest's 'Screaming for Vengeance' in 1982 was their commercial breakthrough, reaching number 17 on the Billboard 200.
Tonewoods matter in metal — mahogany bodies provide warm, thick tones while alder and basswood offer more balanced, scooped sounds.
Meshuggah's Tomas Haake is considered one of the most innovative metal drummers, using polyrhythmic patterns that create the illusion of changing tempos.
The MXR Phase 90 phaser pedal was used by Eddie Van Halen on early Van Halen albums to create his distinctive modulated clean tones.
Brought to their knees: Black Sabbath's 'Iron Man' riff is one of the most recognizable in all of music and is typically the second riff new guitarists learn after 'Smoke on the Water.'
Matt Pike of Sleep and High on Fire plays custom First Act and Electrical Guitar Company aluminum-neck guitars for maximum sustain.
The Gibson SG Standard was first produced in 1961 as a replacement for the Les Paul, and its lighter weight and double-cutaway design made it a favorite for Angus Young and Tony Iommi.
Marshall's 1959 Super Lead 100-watt head, known as the 'Plexi,' is the amp that defined the sound of early heavy metal and hard rock.
Ibanez RG550, reissued in 2018, is a quintessential shred guitar with an Edge tremolo, basswood body, and maple neck designed for speed.
Cedar Creek Recording Studio in Austin was where Pantera recorded 'Vulgar Display of Power' with producer Terry Date.
ESP's Horizon series features a set-through neck construction that provides the sustain of a neck-through with some of the snap of a bolt-on.
Dunlop Tortex picks in the sharp .88mm (green) and 1.0mm (blue) are among the most popular gauges for metal rhythm guitar.
Lemmy Kilmister died on December 28, 2015, just four days after being diagnosed with an aggressive form of cancer.
The Boss NS-2 Noise Suppressor is the most common noise gate pedal used on metal pedalboards to eliminate feedback between riffs.
In 1986, Metallica bassist Cliff Burton was killed when the band's tour bus overturned in Sweden.
The Jackson Pro Series Soloist features a compound radius fingerboard and direct mount pickups, designed for fast, aggressive metal playing.
Disturbed's cover of Simon & Garfunkel's 'The Sound of Silence' reached number one on the Billboard Hard Rock chart and went viral with over a billion views.
Gibson's Dirty Fingers humbucker pickup was specifically designed for maximum output and aggression, aimed squarely at metal players.
The concept of 'tone stacking' in metal involves layering multiple guitar tracks — often four or more — to create a massive wall of sound in recordings.
Judas Priest's 'Electric Eye' features one of the most iconic opening riffs in metal, driven by a harmonized guitar line.
Drop A tuning requires extremely heavy gauge strings, typically .013 or .014 on the low string, to maintain adequate string tension.
Periphery's Misha Mansoor popularized the term 'djent' through online guitar forums and home recordings before forming the band.
The Electro-Harmonix Big Muff fuzz pedal has been used in doom and stoner metal by bands like Electric Wizard and Boris.
Iron Maiden bassist Steve Harris founded the band in 1975 in Leyton, East London, naming it after the medieval torture device.
The '4 cable method' is a complex pedalboard routing technique that places some effects before the amp and others in the effects loop for optimal tone.
Bullet for My Valentine's 'The Poison' went gold in the US in 2006, making it one of the first Welsh metal albums to achieve that milestone.
Schecter guitars, especially the C-1 series, offer premium features at mid-range prices and have become a dominant brand in modern metal.
Mercyful Fate's King Diamond pioneered the use of elaborate corpse paint and theatrical falsetto vocals in heavy metal.
Mike Portnoy's Tama drum kit with Dream Theater featured over 50 pieces including multiple bass drums, toms, and cymbals.
The natural harmonic drone technique, holding a harmonic while playing other notes, is used in post-metal by bands like ISIS and Russian Circles.
Slash of Guns N' Roses plays a Gibson Les Paul Standard through a Marshall JCM800, creating one of the most imitated guitar tones in rock and metal.
Body Cavity Search tuning — an extremely low tuning used by some doom bands — can go as low as drop F or even lower.
The guitar rig used by Adam Jones of Tool consists of a Silverburst Gibson Les Paul through a Diezel VH4 and Marshall amps.
Killswitch Engage's 'The End of Heartache' won the Metal Hammer Golden God Award for Best Song in 2005.
Feedback, the sustained howl produced when an electric guitar is held close to a loud amplifier, is used intentionally in metal for dramatic effect.
Babymetal fused J-pop with heavy metal, attracting both metalheads and pop fans and headlining arenas worldwide.
Signature guitar pedals like the Kirk Hammett Khdk Ghoul Screamer and Zakk Wylde MXR Berzerker Overdrive put pro tones in players' hands.
The classic rock drum kit — kick, snare, hi-hat, two toms, and two crashes — was expanded in metal to include double kick, china cymbals, and splash cymbals.
Whitechapel and Thy Art Is Murder helped popularize deathcore, which combines death metal's extremity with hardcore breakdowns.
Scale length affects string tension — a 25.5-inch Fender scale feels tighter than a 24.75-inch Gibson scale in the same tuning, relevant for metal drop tunings.
Ministry's 'Psalm 69' in 1992 brought industrial metal to the mainstream, peaking at number 27 on the Billboard 200.
Lacquer vs satin neck finishes is a debated topic among metal guitarists, with many preferring satin for its smoother feel during fast playing.
Keith Merrow is a YouTube-era metal guitarist who influenced guitar gear culture through demo videos that popularized brands like Schecter and Mesa Boogie.
The Kemper Profiling Amp captures the tone of real amplifiers digitally, allowing touring metal bands to carry one unit instead of multiple heavy amps.
Anthrax's Joey Belladonna and John Bush represent two eras of the band's vocal style — Belladonna's classic metal and Bush's heavier approach.
The guitar solo is considered sacred in traditional metal, unlike some nu-metal bands of the late 1990s that intentionally excluded solos.
Fear Factory blended industrial metal with death metal vocals, pioneering the clean/scream vocal dynamic that became standard in metalcore.
Trem-bar harmonics — hitting a natural harmonic while simultaneously using the whammy bar — create sci-fi-like squeals popularized by Dimebag Darrell and Joe Satriani.
The Peavey Invective amplifier, co-designed with Misha Mansoor, represents the latest evolution of high-gain metal amps from Peavey.
Loudness from Japan were one of the first Asian metal bands to achieve international recognition in the 1980s.
Machine Head's Robb Flynn helped develop the ENGL Invader amplifier, which became one of the most popular heads in modern metal.
Ola Englund, guitarist of The Haunted, became one of the most influential YouTube gear reviewers for metal equipment.
Crowbar and Down, both featuring Phil Anselmo, helped define the sludge metal sound that emerged from the New Orleans metal scene in the 1990s.
Pick angle and attack — whether picking parallel or at an angle to the strings — significantly affects the brightness and aggression of metal guitar tone.
Deep Purple hold a 1972 Guinness World Record for being the world's loudest band at 117 dB, which Manowar later surpassed.
Locking Floyd Rose bridges require special string changes and setup, but provide unmatched tuning stability for extreme whammy bar use in metal.
The Ibanez Universe 7-string guitar, introduced in 1990, was the first mass-produced 7-string electric guitar and popularized extended range in metal.
